coredata.dc:title: A highly sensitive and selective ratiometric sensing platform based on 7-amino-4-methylcoumarin for naked-eye visual fluorescence sensing of Cu2+
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Sun, Xiaoyan
Guo, Yuxin
Wen, Ruizhi
Li, Hongchang
coredata.dc:description: Cu2+ is a major environmental pollutant. An efficient measurement for Cu2+ is urgently needed. In this report, we have developed a new sensitive and selective ratiometric sensing platform using 7-amino-4-methylcoumarin (AMC) for detecting Cu2+ in real samples. In the presence of Cu2+, o-phenylenediamine (OPD) could be catalytically oxidized to 2,3-diaminophenazine (DAP), which could react with AMC, leading to quench the fluorescence intensity of AMC at 438 nm. Meanwhile, DAP provided a new emission peak at 557 nm. Based on the efficient overlapped spectrum of AMC and DAP, a ratiometric sensing platform through fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) was carried out. Furthermore, the as-proposed strategy displayed the linear relationship in the wide range from 6 to 250 μM with a low detection limit of 0.059 μM, and the recoveries of the spiked samples in real samples ranged from 86.5% to 110.1%. Moreover, comparing the visual fluorescence colors of the real samples with the standard colorimetric card, we used the as-proposed strategy as a solid-based platform for realizing an efficient semi-quantitative detection of Cu2+ via naked-eye visual fluorescence mode without any complicated instrument and operation. The above results implied that the as-proposed strategy could be used in the practice determination of Cu2+.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ratiometric sensing platform
7-Amino-4-methylcoumarin
2,3-Diaminophenazine
Visual fluorescence detection
Cu2+ detection

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: A dual-signal ratiometric sensor for the detection of Cu2+
The selectivity of Cu2+ determination by AMC based ratiometric sensor
For Cu2+ sensing by AMC based ratiometric sensor in real samples
Possible sensing mechanism for AMC based ratiometric sensor
Sensitivity of the AMC based ratiometric sensor for sensing Cu2+
Selectivity of the AMC based ratiometric sensor for sensing Cu2+
Detection of Cu2+ ions in real samples using AMC based ratiometric sensing platform

Central South University of Forestry and Technology
Natural Science Foundation of Hunan Province

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the Natural Science Foundation of Hunan Province ( 2021JJ41064 ), the Department of Education of Hunan Province ( 18C0248 ), and the Research Fund of Central South University of Forestry and Technology ( 2016YJ054 ).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) Fluorescence emission spectra of AMC, AMC + Cu2+, AMC + OPD and AMC + Cu2+ + OPD, respectively. (B) Effects of AMC with the increase of Cu2+ concentration. (C) UV–vis absorption spectra of Cu2+, OPD, Cu2+ + OPD and DAP. (D) Fluorescence emission spectra of OPD reacted with different concentrations of Cu2+ under 420 nm excitation wavelength. (E) the optical absorption spectrum of Cu2+ + OPD and fluorescence emission spectrum of AMC. The inset are the images of AMC (left) and Cu2+ + OPD (right) under a 365 nm UV lamp. (F) Fluorescence lifetime of AMC and AMC + Cu2+ + OPD.

Optimal conditions for preparing the as-proposed ratiometric sensing platform. Effects of molar ratio of AMC and OPD in the presence of Cu2+ (from left to right: the concentration of OPD were 10, 20, 30, 40 and 50 mM, respectively.) (A), solution pH value (B), reaction temperature (C) and reaction time (D) on the ratio changes (F557/F438) of fluorescence intensity of ratiometric sensor. The error bars represent the standard deviation of three independent measurements.

(A) Fluorescence spectra of the ratiometric sensing platform with different concentration of Cu2+ from 0 to 400 μM under 360 nm excitation light. (B) The curves of ratiometric fluorescence intensity (F557/F438) with different concentration of Cu2+ (0–400 μM). Inset: The linear relationship of ratiometric fluorescence intensity (F557/F438) vs. the added concentration of Cu2+ (6–250 μM). The error bar represented the standard deviation of three independent measurements.

(A) The ratio changes (F557/F438) of the present ratiometric sensing platform toward various metal ions at physiological pH. Samples marked with 1–12 corresponding to blank, Cu2+, Na+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Sn2+, Zn2+, Pb2+, Fe3+ and Ba2+, respectively. (B) The ratio changes (F557/F438) of the present ratiometric sensing platform after adding various metal ions in the presence of Cu2+ at physiological pH. (from 1 to 11: blank, Na+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Cd2+, Sn2+, Zn2+, Pb2+, Fe3+ and Ba2+) The concentration of Cu2+ was 100 μM, other metal concentration was 500 μM. The error bars represent the standard deviation of three independent measurements.

Visual fluorescence detection of Cu2+ in real samples using the present ratiometric sensing platform. The Cu2+-spiked sensing system was placed into the centrifuge tubes or wetting filter papers under excitation wavelength of a 365 UV lamp.
Schematic representation of the as-proposed ratiometric sensing platform based on AMC for Cu2+ ions detection.
Detection of Cu2+ in lake water and tap water samples via the as-proposed ratiometric sensing platform.

head.ce:title: A highly sensitive and selective ratiometric sensing platform based on 7-amino-4-methylcoumarin for naked-eye visual fluorescence sensing of Cu2+

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Science, Central South University of Forestry and Technology, Changsha 410004, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Central South University of Forestry and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Science, Central South University of Forestry and Technology, Changsha 410004, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: China Certification & Inspection Group Hunan Co., Ltd, Changsha 410021, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: China Certification & Inspection Group Hunan Co., Ltd

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: China Certification & Inspection Group Hunan Co., Ltd, Changsha 410021, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Inspired by the efficiently spectrum overlap between AMC and DAP, integrating two kinds of fluorescent probe in one system to develop a high-performance ratiometric sensing platform based on FRET mechanism for detecting Cu2+ via naked-eye visual fluorescence mode in real samples has been constructed.

Cu2+ is a major environmental pollutant. An efficient measurement for Cu2+ is urgently needed. In this report, we have developed a new sensitive and selective ratiometric sensing platform using 7-amino-4-methylcoumarin (AMC) for detecting Cu2+ in real samples. In the presence of Cu2+, o-phenylenediamine (OPD) could be catalytically oxidized to 2,3-diaminophenazine (DAP), which could react with AMC, leading to quench the fluorescence intensity of AMC at 438 nm. Meanwhile, DAP provided a new emission peak at 557 nm. Based on the efficient overlapped spectrum of AMC and DAP, a ratiometric sensing platform through fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) was carried out. Furthermore, the as-proposed strategy displayed the linear relationship in the wide range from 6 to 250 μM with a low detection limit of 0.059 μM, and the recoveries of the spiked samples in real samples ranged from 86.5% to 110.1%. Moreover, comparing the visual fluorescence colors of the real samples with the standard colorimetric card, we used the as-proposed strategy as a solid-based platform for realizing an efficient semi-quantitative detection of Cu2+ via naked-eye visual fluorescence mode without any complicated instrument and operation. The above results implied that the as-proposed strategy could be used in the practice determination of Cu2+.

ce:section.ce:para: Organic fluorescent small molecules, such as rhodamine, fluorescein, coumarin, cyanine, because of the unique structure and property, have received increasing interest and have been applied in numerous fields, especially in environmental analytical chemistry [1,2]. For example, coumarin and its derivatives, as important organic fluorescent small molecules, are commonly found in the nature of rutaceae, umbelliferaceae, legumes, orchids, etc. Since the scientists have synthesized coumarin-like fluorescent molecules with different structures, possessing high fluorescence quantum yield, large stoke shift, strong optical stability, therefore the as-prepared coumarin-like fluorescent molecules as fluorescent probe were widely used in biosensing, bioimaging, and so on [3–6].

As far as we know, single fluorophore as an output for detecting related analytes might be influence by some factors, e.g., instrumental efficiency, environmental conditions, as well as the concentration of fluorescent probes. These disadvantages could be inhibited by ratiometric sensing platform, which provided an environmental interference correction and eliminated the fluctuation of light excitation intensity [7–9]. Ratiometric sensing platform, integrating two kinds of fluorescent probes in one system by measuring the ratio of two emissions, is of prime importance for achieving an accurate assay with highly sensitive and selective. By establishing an internal standard, it is self-regulating and greatly reduces the interference of many variables or unknowns such as ambient temperature, probe concentration, solution polarity and pH value [10]. Compared with traditional fluorescence detection manner, ratiometric sensing platform has some remarkable advantages, including simple pretreatment, high sensitivity and selectivity, as well as realization of visual detection via naked-eye. Visual detection is a kind of method that relies on the naked eye to directly interpret the detection results, which usually utilize the unique properties of some materials through physical and/or chemical reactions to change the solution color for reaching the purpose of detection [11,12]. Therefore, visual detection has some advantages, such as instrument-free dependence, low-cost, benefiting for on-site detection. etc. In recent years, gold nanoparticles with the unique physical and chemical properties have been widely used in the construction of visual colorimetric probes, which have been favored by many researchers in the fields of nucleic acid detection, protein analysis and small molecule detection [13]. But the whole detection process is carried out in solution, which partly limits the application of visual detection. It is expected to develop a small, portable on-site equipment by converting liquid-based platform to solid-based platform (e.g., filter paper, strip, thin film, etc.) [14]. For example, paper-based devices as solid-based platform are widely used in visual detection because of their small size, long-term stability, and easy to operate and carry. Consequently, the semi-quantitative or qualitative analysis of the analytes can be carried out using naked-eye visual fluorescence detection mode via solid-based platform [15]. Based on this merit, ratiometric sensing platform has gained more and more research interest. In order to expand the fundamental studies and applications of ratiometric sensing platform in numerous fields, seeking new fluorescent materials for building ratiometric sensing platform is necessary [16–19].

It is well known that Cu2+ is the third essential transition metal element, which plays a vital role in regulating various physiological activities of the human body [20–22]. For instance, it can serve as an important cofactor for many enzymes. While it is also an environmental pollutant with high toxicity and bioaccumulative properties [23]. If people over-take Cu2+ in tap water or other copper-containing materials, which can excessively accumulate in human body for a long time, leading to some serious diseases such as cell damage of apoptosis [24,25], liver and kidney damage [26], and central nervous system damage (e.g., Alzheimer’s disease and Wilson’s disease) [27–29]. So we should pay more attention to the issue about copper contamination, then the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) defines the safe concentration of Cu2+ in drinking water at 1.3 μg/mL (ca. 20 μM). Therefore, the determination of Cu2+ in tap water or human body is of importance for the prevention of diseases. Toward this goal, there are many existing methods for determination of Cu2+, containing atomic fluorescence spectrometry (AFS), atomic absorption/emission spectroscopy (AAS or AES), inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS), high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) [30], electrochemistry [31], colorimetric sensing [32], fluorescence [19,33,34], and so on. Among these strategies, the traditional assay such as AAS and HPLC, due to expensive instruments and/or complicated sample pretreatment process, have limited the applications of detecting Cu2+ in real samples. Different from the traditional assay, fluorescent analysis especially ratiometric fluorescent sensor owing to high sensitivity, facile and eco-effective operation, has been commonly adopted.

Inspired by the efficiently spectrum overlap between AMC and DAP. a novel dual-signal ratiometric fluorescent probe based on AMC for sensing Cu2+ via catalytically induced FRET mechanism was developed (Scheme 1 ). Yellow fluorescent DAP centered at 557 nm was formed in situ derived from the conversion of OPD in the presence of Cu2+, because of the formation of DAP, the emission intensity of AMC centered at 438 nm was dramatically decreased due to the FRET mechanism. Integrating two kinds of fluorescent probe to one system for establishing a dual-fluorescence response strategy not only enabled self-correction of detection but also improved sensitivity of sensing. Based on the specific response, the as-proposed strategy could be used for the detection of Cu2+ in aqueous samples and the naked-eye visual fluorescence detection of Cu2+ on filter paper.

All the chemicals and solvents were of at least analytical grade and commercially available. 7-amino-4-methylcoumarin (AMC) was purchased from Sigma-Aldrich chemical Co. (St. louis, MO). Tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (Tris) buffer was purchased from Macklin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd (Shanghai, China). o-phenylenediamine (OPD), hydrochloric acid (HCl) and sodium hydroxide (NaOH) were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Beijing, China). 2,3-diaminophenazine (DAP) was purchased from Aladdin Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd (Shanghai, China). Water (greater than18.2 M Ω) used for the experiments was purified by Milli-Q System. The solutions of Cu2+, K+, Na+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Ba2+, Cd2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, Sn2+and Fe3+ were prepared from CuSO4, KCl, NaCl, MgCl2, CaCl2, BaCl2, CdCl2, PbCl2, ZnCl2, SnCl2 and FeCl3, respectively.

Absorption spectra were recorded using a UV-3310 UV–vis spectrophotometer (Hitachi, Japan). UV light with excitation wavelength at 365 nm was used. Fluorescence emission spectra were recorded using an F-4500 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Hitachi, Japan), the emission spectra were recorded using 10 nm/10 nm slit widths. The fluorescence decay measurement was recorded using a Quantaurus-Tau compact fluorescence lifetime spectrometer (Hamamatsu photonics, C11367-31).

Both AMC and OPD were dissolved in 0.2 M, pH 7.4 Tris-HCl buffer solution, and their concentrations were 50 μM and 50 mM, respectively. It should be noted that these solutions needed to be prepared freshly. 0.2 M Tris-HCl solution (pH 7 ∼ 9) was obtained through adjusting the molar ratio of Tris and HCl.

ce:section.ce:section-title: A dual-signal ratiometric sensor for the detection of Cu2+
ce:section.ce:para: All glasswares used in the experiments were cleaned in a bath of freshly prepared aqua regia (3:1 HCl/HNO3) and rinsed thoroughly in pure water prior to use. In this assay, copper sulfate anhydrate (CuSO4) was chosen as the Cu2+ resource. A CuSO4 stock solution was prepared, and then various concentration (0-–300 μM) was obtained by serial dilution of the stock solution. For sensing Cu2+, 100 μL of various Cu2+ concentration by dilution of stock solution was adequately mixed with AMC (20 μL, 50 μM), OPD (50 μL, 50 mM) and Tris-HCl (830 μL, 0.2 M, pH 7.4) at 37 °C for 2 min in a 2 mL centrifuge tube, and then, the above mixtures were incubated at 37 °C in a water bath for 2 h. The ratio changes (F557/F438) of fluorescence intensity of ratiometric sensor were recorded under an excitation wavelength of 360 nm using F-4500 fluorescence spectrophotometer.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The selectivity of Cu2+ determination by AMC based ratiometric sensor
ce:section.ce:para: To obtain the specificity of AMC based ratiometric sensor for detecting Cu2+, the reaction solution contained 20 μL of 50 μM AMC, 50 μL of 50 mM OPD and 830 μL of 0.2 M Tris-HCl buffer solution (PH = 7.4) in a 2 mL centrifuge tube, and was kept at 37 °C in a water bath for 2 min; then 100 μL of different metal ions, such as K+, Na+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Ba2+, Cd2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, Sn2+, Fe3+, were added to the reaction solution at 37 °C and reacting for 2 h. The ratio changes (F557/F438) of fluorescence intensity of ratiometric sensor were recorded under an excitation wavelength of 360 nm using F-4500 fluorescence spectrophotometer.

ce:section.ce:section-title: For Cu2+ sensing by AMC based ratiometric sensor in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: The real samples containing lake water and tap water, were collected from Xi Lake, Meixi Lake, Ailai Lake, and the lab. of college of science in Central South University of Forestry and Technology, respectively. The obtained samples were filtered with 0.22 μm microporous membrane, and then the filtrate was applied for sensing Cu2+ according to general procedure without any additional pretreatment. For detecting Cu2+ in real samples, i.e., lake water and tap water, 100 μL of the real samples was adequately mixed with AMC (20 μL, 50 μM), OPD (50 μL, 50 mM) and Tris-HCl (830 μL, 0.2 M, pH 7.4) at 37 °C for 2 min in a 2 mL centrifuge tube, and then, the above mixtures were incubated at 37 °C in a water bath for 2 h. The ratio changes (F557/F438) of fluorescence intensity of ratiometric sensor were recorded under an excitation wavelength of 360 nm using F-4500 fluorescence spectrophotometer.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Possible sensing mechanism for AMC based ratiometric sensor
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the possible fluorescent quenching mechanism of AMC induced by DAP, a series of analytical measurements have been carried out. As shown in Fig. 1 -A and B, neither Cu2+ nor OPD alone could quench the fluorescence intensity of AMC, to eliminate the influence of Cu2+ on the emission intensity of AMC, a various concentration of Cu2+ was added into the solution of AMC. Even upon addition to 300 μM, there was no obvious change on emission intensity of AMC. While Cu2+ and OPD together could quench the fluorescence intensity of AMC (Fig. 1-A), possibly due to in situ formation of DAP by catalytically induced conversion of OPD in the presence of Cu2+ [35,36]. To further prove the interaction mechanism between AMC and DAP, as demonstrated in Fig. 1-C, the ultraviolet absorption spectra of Cu2+ and OPD indicated that no apparent surface plasmon resonance absorption peak centered at 420 nm, whereas upon addition of Cu2+, OPD could be catalytically oxidized to form a novel product, whose surface plasmon resonance absorption peak centered at 420 nm. This result was consistent with the optical absorption spectrum of DAP, so the novel product was considered as DAP. This phenomenon was similar to the oxidation of OPD by Ag+ in the reported literature [16,37]. In Fig. 1-D, with the increase of the concentration of Cu2+, the fluorescence intensity of DAP localized at 557 nm was dramatically increased under optimal excitation wavelength of 420 nm. This result indicated that more and more DAP molecules were in situ formed due to the Cu2+ catalytical oxidation, which enabled the emission from AMC to quench efficiently. It is well known that an efficient spectral overlap between the donor and acceptor as a precondition should be considered for developing a high performance ratiometric sensing platform. Fortunately, as depicted in Fig. 1-E, there was a large extent overlapped spectrum between the optical absorption spectrum of DAP and the fluorescence emission spectrum of AMC. Accordingly, a high-performance ratiometric sensing platform for sensing Cu2+ had been established based on energy transfer (ET), in which DAP served as donor and AMC was acceptor. In order to distinguish the reaction mechanism between FRET and inner-filter effect (IFE), the curves of fluorescence lifetime needed to be carried out. As shown in Fig. 1-F, the fluorescence lifetime of AMC was significantly changed in the absence and presence of Cu2+-OPD system, suggesting that the mechanism of AMC based high performance ratiometric sensing platform was FRET instead of IFE. Because the result was different from the mechanism of IFE, where an unchanged fluorescence lifetime was often observed [38,39]. Therefore, the formed DAP through in-situ conversion of OPD in the presence of Cu2+ could react with AMC, leading to quench the fluorescence intensity of AMC centered at 438 nm, meanwhile, DAP provided a new emission peak at 557 nm. By virtue of this merit, integrating AMC and formed DAP in one system to develop a high performance ratiometric sensing platform for detecting Cu2+ had been constructed.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensitivity of the AMC based ratiometric sensor for sensing Cu2+
ce:section.ce:para: Benefiting from the efficient reaction between AMC and formed DAP based on FRET mechanism, the present ratiometric sensing platform was used for the detection of Cu2+. To obtain the feasibility of the as-proposed ratiometric sensing platform, the influence of some parameters, i.e., the molar ratio of AMC and OPD, the solution pH, reaction temperature, reaction time, could be investigated. As shown in Fig. 2 -A, when the concentration of AMC remained unchanged, with the increase of the concentration of OPD, more and more DAP formed in situ, confirming that the emission intensity at 557 was increased, subsequently the ratio (F557/F438) was enhanced at the same time. While the concentration of OPD remained unchanged, with the increase of the concentration of AMC, the result was opposite of the previous situation, the ratio of F557/F438 was dramatically decreased. Taking many factors into consideration, the molar ratio of AMC and OPD (50 μM/50 mM) was chosen. Fig. 2-B depicted that the influence of the solution pH toward the present ratiometric sensing platform. As shown in the picture, the ratio of F557/F438 was higher at pH 7.8 than other pH value. However, because of the presence of carbonic acid (H2CO3), sodium hydrogen carbonate (NaHCO3), blood plasma protein and hemoglobin in blood, which can maintain the solution pH at ca. 7.4. This pH value is most suitable for cell metabolism and body survival, and the ratio of F557/F438 at pH 7.4 was nearly no difference with the pH of 7.8. To apply for further sensing Cu2+ in cell or vivo, so we chose the solution pH value was 7.4. We further investigate the reaction temperature and time for sensing Cu2+ using the present ratiometric sensing platform. The AMC molecules could react with DAP through in situ conversion of OPD in the presence of Cu2+, as shown in Fig. 2-C, the ratio of F557/F438 was highest at 37 °C. From the Fig. 2-D, the ratio of fluorescence intensity increased gradually after 30 min, and then the reaction was the fastest from 60 min to 120 min, When the reaction reached 120 min, the fluorescence intensity ratio increased slowly with the reaction time increasing. Thus, 120 min was set as the optimum time for the reaction system.

As we know, OPD could be catalytically oxidized by Cu2+, in which the product DAP emitted yellow fluorescence under an excitation wavelength of 420 nm. Under the optimal conditions, with the increase of the added concentration of Cu2+, the fluorescence intensity of DAP centered at 557 nm could be remarkably increased. By virtue of this merit, we could use single OPD for sensing Cu2+ without AMC molecules. As depicted in Fig. S1, there was a good linear relationship between the freshly formed DAP and the concentration of added Cu2+ over the range from 10 to 150 μM, and the corresponding linear function was F557 = 23.0759C + 773.8999 with a correlation coefficient of R2 = 0.997. The limit of detection (LOD) was 1.482 μM (S/N = 3). Whereas, if the reaction solution contained AMC molecules, the freshly formed DAP through in situ conversion of OPD in the presence of Cu2+ could react with AMC, which leaded to quench the fluorescence intensity of AMC as well as provided a new emission peak at 557 nm under an excitation wavelength of 360 nm, forming a high performance ratiometric sensing platform. Based on the above situation, we used the as-prepared high performance ratiometric sensing platform for detecting Cu2+ under the optimal conditions. Fig. 3 shown that the linear relationship between the F557/F438 value and the added Cu2+ concentration was F557/F438 = 0.00655C + 0.11416 in the range of 6–250 μM with a correlation coefficient of R2 = 0.995. the LOD was 0.059 μM (S/N = 3), which was relatively lower than that of previous reports, and the detailed comparison was presented in Table S1. Comparing with the strategy that included single OPD by Cu2+, the ratiometric fluorescent method based on AMC improved the sensitivity of Cu2+ more than 25 times.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity of the AMC based ratiometric sensor for sensing Cu2+
ce:section.ce:para: To prove the specificity of the present ratiometric sensing platform for the determination of Cu2+ concentration, the fluorescence response of the present ratiometric sensing platform toward a series of metal ions including Na+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Sn2+, Pb2+, Zn2+, Ba2+, Fe3+ was evaluated under the same conditions. From Fig. 4 -A, it was obvious that only Cu2+ ions could reconcile OPD with AMC at physiological pH, forming a significant ratiometric sensing platform, while other metal ions such as K+ could not do that. Even Cu2+ ions coexisting with other metal ions, e.g., Na+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, and so on, the results were consisted with above situation (Fig. 4-B). In brief, the high selectivity of the present ratiometric sensing platform indicated that it could be applied for sensing Cu2+ ions in complex systems.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of Cu2+ ions in real samples using AMC based ratiometric sensing platform
ce:section.ce:para: To validate the practicality of the proposed strategy, we used the present ratiometric sensing platform based on analyte catalytically induced FRET mechanism for highly sensitive and selective detection of Cu2+ ions in real samples. The measured results of Cu2+ in tap water and lake water through the present ratiometric sensing platform were listed in Table 1 . The recoveries of the spiked samples by the proposed strategy ranged from 86.5% to 110.1%. While Cu2+ concentrations determined by use of a commercial assay kit were listed in Table S2. We could conclude that two approaches were not of significant difference. We further investigated the fluorescence color changes of the as-proposed strategy by naked eyes in real samples. In general, the visual fluorescence color changes of aqueous solution are conveniently observed by naked eyes, compared with single fluorophore with visual fluorescence color changes, the visual fluorescence color changes of the dual-readout emitting ratiometric sensing platform could realize a rapid, convenient and efficient semi-quantitative detection of analytes. As illustrated in Fig. 5 , the Cu2+-spiked sensing system was placed into the centrifuge tubes or wetting filter papers, with the increase of added Cu2+ ions from 0 to 100 μM (top of the photograph), the related visual fluorescence colors gradually changed from deep blue to deep yellow under excitation wavelength of a 365 nm UV lamp, which could be set as standard colorimetric card. According to comparing the visual fluorescence colors of the real samples with the standard colorimetric card, we could realize a convenient semi-quantitative detection of Cu2+ without any complicated instrument and operation (bottom of the photograph). It is well-known that the solid-based platform (such as filter paper, strips, thin film, etc.) has wider application prospect than the liquid-based platform. The above results implied that the present ratiometric probe as the solid-based platform has a great feasibility for on-site and naked-eye visual fluorescence detection of Cu2+ in real samples.

In conclusion, a sensitive and selective ratiometric sensing platform based on AMC via FRET mechanism for the detection of Cu2+ in real samples was successfully established. Firstly, OPD could be catalytically oxidized by Cu2+ to generate DAP, which could react with AMC. Inspired by the efficiently overlapped spectra between DAP and AMC, DAP acted as the fluorescence donor while AMC acted as the fluorescence acceptor in this research, with the increase of Cu2+, significant fluorescence ratio changes were observed, together with a reduced blue-emitting fluorescence centered at 438 nm and an increased yellow-emitting fluorescence centered at 557 nm. Based on the specific response, integrating AMC and the formed DAP in one system to construct a high performance ratiometric sensing platform was very essential for sensing Cu2+ in real samples. Furthermore, the present ratiometric sensing platform by measuring the ratio of two emissions not only could reduce systematic errors and interferences from the environment but also improved the sensitivity of detection. Compared with the method that only contained OPD for sensing Cu2+, the AMC based ratiometric sensing platform gains the detection sensitivity by about 25 times. Moreover, through establishing a standard colorimetric card, we could use the present ratiometric probe as a solid-based platform for realizing an efficient semi-quantitative detection of Cu2+ without any complicated instrument and operation via naked-eye visual fluorescence mode. By virtue of these merits, the present ratiometric sensing platform has a great potential for the accurate/on-site detection in a complex environment.

Xiaoyan Sun: Conceptualization, Methodology, Writing – original draft, Writing – review & editing. Yuxin Guo: Investigation, Resources, Writing – original draft. Ruizhi Wen: Supervision. Hongchang Li: Conceptualization, Funding acquisition, Supervision.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the Natural Science Foundation of Hunan Province (2021JJ41064), the Department of Education of Hunan Province (18C0248), and the Research Fund of Central South University of Forestry and Technology (2016YJ054).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2021.120627.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Organic fluorescent probes for monitoring autophagy in living cells
Chem. Soc. Rev.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Li X, Liang X, Yin J, Lin W. Organic fluorescent probes for monitoring autophagy in living cells. Chemical Society Reviews 2021, doi:10.1039/D0CS00896F.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A short review on NIR-II organic small molecule dyes
Dyes Pigments

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A coumarin-based sensitive and selective fluorescent sensor for copper (II) ions
New J. Chem.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A new coumarin-derived fluorescent sensor with red-emission for Zn2+ in aqueous solution
